commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7bc2df3f8c348e7ffb48273378eec4d79b789ea4)
commands.review_summary:
The PR bounds memory-core short-term promotion snippets before writing them to MEMORY.md, exposes a deep-dreaming snippet-token cap through config/status/docs, preserves promotion provenance metadata, and includes small TypeScript build-path repairs.

PR surface: Source +85, Tests +65, Docs +4. Total +154 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main writes the full candidate snippet directly into MEMORY.md, and the PR body shows a disposable-workspace run where a 465-character source snippet became a compact 166-character promoted line with metadata retained.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A maintainer should decide whether the new public config/default and default truncation behavior are acceptable; there is no narrow automation repair that settles that policy choice.

target.title: fix(memory): compact short-term promotion entries
target.body:
## Summary
- Bound visible short-term promotion snippets before appending them to root `MEMORY.md`, so long daily-note ranges cannot be copied wholesale into bootstrap memory.
- Add `dreaming.phases.deep.maxPromotedSnippetTokens` (default 120 estimated tokens) and pass it through config, CLI/status, docs, and apply-time promotion.
- Preserve visible provenance metadata on each promoted entry: `score`, `recalls`, `avg`, and `source`, while keeping marker comments focused on duplicate reconciliation.
- Keep the exported plugin SDK field optional so external TypeScript consumers that construct `MemoryDeepDreamingConfig` do not need to add the new cap on upgrade.
- Rebase on current `origin/main` and repair two existing TypeScript blockers in the plugin SDK d.ts build path.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Short-term promotion compacts the visible promoted daily-note snippet before writing `MEMORY.md`, while preserving the ranking/provenance metadata on the entry.

Real environment tested: Disposable local OpenClaw workspace created under `/tmp/openclaw-memory-proof-*` from this PR branch after rebasing on current `origin/main`.

Exact steps or command run after the patch:
1. Created a temp workspace with `memory/2026-04-01.md` containing one long daily note line.
2. Recorded one short-term recall for that line with score `0.94`.
3. Ranked and applied promotion with `maxPromotedSnippetTokens: 24`.
4. Read the resulting `MEMORY.md` promoted line.

Evidence after fix:
```text
workspace=/tmp/openclaw-memory-proof-CFsa7H
applied=1 appended=1 ranked=1
sourceSnippetChars=465
promotedLineChars=166
- HanJammer reviewed the dashboard state and found that MEMORY.md had received raw promoted daily... [score=0.611 recalls=1 avg=0.940 source=memory/2026-04-01.md:3-3]
```

Observed result after fix: The promoted line was shortened from a 465-character source snippet to a 166-character `MEMORY.md` line, includes an ellipsis, and retains `score`, `recalls`, `avg`, and `source` provenance.

Not tested: No live user workspace was modified; the proof used a disposable local workspace. Full CI is still the source of truth for repository-wide checks.

### Supplemental tests
```text
pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-memory.config.ts extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/config.test.ts
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  106 passed (106)

pnpm tsgo:prod
passed

pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke
passed
```

## Notes
This is a narrow mitigation, not full semantic long-term-memory curation. The current path is deterministic ranking + rehydration + formatting; it does not ask an LLM to distill stable conclusions for `MEMORY.md`. A follow-up should add an explicit LLM/curator stage for durable-memory promotion rather than relying on snippet compaction alone.
target.author: a-m-a-r-a
